language.label: Language
hero.title: OpenClaw Friends
hero.description: This version replaces direct MBTI labels and rough relationship tags with broader personality questions, world context, and an affection arc. The goal is not a personality test, but a more natural starting point for the character and the relationship.
hero.githubHint: The repo is pinned here so people can support it in one click.
hero.githubAria: Open the GitHub repository and star it
hero.videoHint: Watch the full flow from setup to character generation.
hero.whyBody: Not everyone knows their MBTI, but most people know whether they recharge alone or socially, and whether they want direct communication or gentle emotional support. Breaking it down makes both the character and the relationship easier to build.
list.title: Characters
list.description: Pick a character here first, then enter the editor when you want to change it.
stepper.title: Setup Flow
stepper.description: Complete character setup, blueprint review, Discord, and TuQu in order.
step.profile.title: Character Info
step.profile.description: Fill in character info, my profile, and relationship setup
step.details.title: Character Details
step.discord.title: Discord Setup
step.discord.description: Configure channel, user, and bot token
step.tuqu.title: TuQu Setup
step.tuqu.description: Configure Service Key and character ID
panel.characterInfo.title: Character Info
panel.characterInfo.editing: Update the core character info and choose the character's default reply language.
panel.characterInfo.creating: Start with the character's world and vibe, then infer MBTI from broader behavior questions.
panel.user.title: My Info
panel.user.description: This stores stable user info. Changes are auto-saved and reused for future characters.
panel.relationship.title: Our Relationship
panel.relationship.description: These settings belong only to the current character and affect only this character's generation result.
status.userAutoSave: My info auto-saves and will be reused for new characters.
status.userSaved: “My Info” was auto-saved and will be reused for new characters.
status.relationshipOnly: This section belongs only to the current character.
field.name: Name
field.famousCharacterHelp: Tell the model whether this concept should be treated as an existing well-known character.
field.personalityInferenceHelp: When enabled, the four personality axes act as a soft hint. When disabled, the model relies on the concept, world, and relationship inputs instead.
field.communicationPreference: How do you prefer the other side to communicate with you?
field.userNameForRole: What should the character call you?
field.desiredBond: What kind of relationship feeling do you want between you two?
field.treatmentPreference: How would you like to be treated?
field.specialTraits: What traits are you okay with the character having?
placeholder.worldSetting: For example: modern Earth, cultivation world, future city, alternate dynasty
placeholder.concept: Write everything you think about this character here: vibe, tags, relationship fantasy, aesthetics, design notes, anything.
placeholder.famousCharacterSource: For example: Re:Zero, Evangelion, Harry Potter
placeholder.characterNotes: Other notes: for example tsundere, verbally stubborn, slightly possessive, quiet but action-oriented.
placeholder.userNotes: Other notes: for example socially anxious at first but very talkative later, or rational but soft-hearted.
placeholder.userNameForRole: For example: admin, Yizhou, brother, master
placeholder.other: Other: add your own if the options are not enough
placeholder.otherTraits: Other: add any relationship traits you want
profile.footer: Complete the character info first. Clicking next will update the details and sync the workspace.
user.footer: This section auto-saves. No separate save button is needed.
relationship.footer: This section will be saved with the character info on the next step and used to generate the details.
axis.character.socialEnergy: When they're drained, they recover more by
axis.character.decisionStyle: When deciding, they first look at
axis.user.socialEnergy: When you're drained, you recover more by
axis.user.decisionStyle: When deciding, you first look at
wizard.nextDescription: Save the character info and questionnaire, generate the latest character details, and check the workspace. Update it if it exists; create it if it doesn't.
button.nextGenerate: Next: Generate Details and Sync Workspace
status.profileRequired: This step cannot be skipped. Clicking next will save the character, generate the blueprint, and sync the workspace.
status.profileNoChanges: No changes in step one. Jumped directly to character details.
choice.lifeStage.creator: Creator / Freelancer
choice.bond.flirty: Slightly flirty with some push and pull
choice.bond.moreThanFriend: More than friends, not quite lovers
choice.bond.guided: A slight feeling of being guided
choice.treatment.cared: Be cared for in daily life
choice.treatment.caught: Be caught when I need support
choice.treatment.reminded: Be reminded and pushed a bit
choice.trait.coolSlow: Cool and slow to warm up
status.readyCreate: Ready to create a new character.
status.workspaceImported: Imported workspace character “{name}”. You can now edit it and fill in the missing config.
empty.noCharacters: No characters yet. Click “New Character” in the top right.
empty.noBlueprint: This character does not have generated details yet. Go back to the previous step, save the basics, and generate the blueprint first.
discord.description: Save the channel, user, and bot token here. You can continue directly or skip this step.
discord.portalDescription: Open the Discord developer portal in a new window, create a New Application, then come back here to fill in the bot token and IDs.
discord.noWorkspace: This character does not have a workspace yet.
discord.completeProfileFirst: Finish step one first, then save Discord config for this character.
discord.footer: Click “Next” to save the Discord config, or “Skip” to move past this step.
discord.repairHint: If channel @mentions stop working, click “Repair OpenClaw Registration” to safely rebuild the agent, bindings, and channel routing.
discord.placeholderServerId: Optional; if empty, it will try to resolve it from the Channel ID
discord.placeholderChannelId: Target channel for OpenClaw binding; @mention is still required in the current takeover mode
discord.placeholderUserId: Your Discord user ID; only this account can interact with the character
discord.placeholderBotToken: Login token for this character bot
status.discordRepairDone: OpenClaw registration repaired. agent={agentId}, account={accountId}, guild={guildId}.
tuqu.description: Save the TuQu Service Key and character ID here. Finishing this step returns to the homepage.
tuqu.noWorkspace: This character does not have a workspace yet.
tuqu.footer: Click “Finish Setup” to save the TuQu config and return home, or “Skip” to end the flow now.
tuqu.characterInfo: A TuQu Character is a reference character created from the selected role's photos and traits. Once created, you can keep generating photos for that role without sending extra parameters beyond the character ID.
runtime.title: Discord Bots
runtime.description: After you save Discord config in the setup flow, OpenClaw will take over bot runtime and @mention routing for all characters. The local controls below are only for debugging and run the bots inside the Designer process instead of through OpenClaw.
runtime.noneSaved: No character-level Discord accounts have been saved yet. Enter the editor flow and finish Discord setup first.
runtime.localDebug: Local Debug: Run Bots inside Designer
runtime.running: At least one Discord bot is currently running locally.
runtime.notRunning: No Discord bots are currently running locally.
workspacePicker.title: Import Existing Workspace
workspacePicker.description: Choose an existing workspace and import it as a Designer character. After import, you can edit it, fill in missing config, and sync it back to the workspace.
workspacePicker.empty: No importable workspaces were found. Workspaces already managed by the Designer are hidden here.
detail.title: Character Details
detail.descriptionWizard: Review the generated result and edit the markdown if needed before continuing.
detail.descriptionBrowse: After generation, you can review the structured blueprint and keep editing the character details.
detail.mbtiInsufficient: The current axes are still not stable enough to infer a solid MBTI.
detail.personalityInferenceDisabledHelp: This run will not force MBTI or axis-based framing. The model will prioritize the concept and known setting instead.
detail.markdownHint: You can edit all four markdown files directly here. Saving will overwrite the final content in the character package.
detail.workspaceAfterProfile: The workspace will be created and synced automatically after step one.
